mechanism: R-star uncertainty determines whether current equity multiples are fairly priced or 15-20% overvalued. If r-star settles at the higher end of the HLW confidence interval, the Nifty Fifty discount rate shock precedent activates as multiple compression becomes necessary.
regime_dependence: Activates if r-star resolves above 1.5% and inflation regime shifts durably above 2.5%. Dormant if AI productivity keeps r-star low.

mechanism: Passive investing at 55% AUM means all historical base rates were estimated in a structurally different regime. This compounds the existing small-sample problem: not only are samples too small for claimed precision, but the generating process itself has changed.
regime_dependence: Applies to all cycle analyses. Severity increases with passive share; currently at historically unprecedented levels.

mechanism: The fiscal multiplier being 0.3-0.6 at positive output gaps directly haircuts the Kalecki channel's EPS contribution from the headline $20-40/share to approximately $15-25/share, thinning the fiscal floor.
regime_dependence: Multiplier is state-dependent: 0.3-0.6 at positive output gaps, 1.0-2.5 in recession. Kalecki channel strengthens precisely when the economy weakens.

mechanism: Both represent physical input constraints on AI capex execution. Copper constrains data center and grid infrastructure buildout; natural gas constrains power generation costs. Together they form a two-pronged supply bottleneck.
regime_dependence: Binding when AI capex growth exceeds 15-20% annually. Loosens if AI investment decelerates or geographic diversification occurs.

mechanism: Correlation flickering between positive and negative regimes creates ~200bp swings in 60/40 portfolio volatility, making short-duration IG (which avoids the correlation instability) relatively more attractive as a portfolio diversifier.
regime_dependence: Sharpe advantage is most pronounced during correlation instability. If correlation durably settles negative (as in 2000-2020), traditional 60/40 diversification restores and IG advantage narrows.

mechanism: Mag7 loading simultaneously on quality, profitability, momentum, and low-vol creates a single concentrated bet. A discount rate shock (per Nifty Fifty precedent) would cause simultaneous drawdowns across all factor portfolios, amplifying the decline beyond what diversified factor exposure would suggest.
regime_dependence: Active while Mag7 constitutes >30% of cap-weighted indices and simultaneously loads on multiple factors. Deactivates if leadership broadens.

mechanism: Because the size premium is actually a credit premium, and the value universe is populated by the same low-quality, high-leverage companies, both factors are trapped in the same structural position. Buying value or small-cap mechanically sells quality in the current regime.
regime_dependence: Active while HML-RMW correlation is -0.3 to -0.5 and Russell 2000 composition skews toward unprofitable, leveraged firms. Could break if rate cuts improve credit quality broadly.

mechanism: A flat Phillips Curve means output gaps produce minimal inflation, allowing the cycle to extend without triggering the inflation → rate hike → recession sequence. This stretches the Stage 1 to peak timing beyond historical norms but creates nonlinear re-steepening tail risk.
regime_dependence: Active at current Phillips slope of 0.10-0.15. If slope re-steepens (as in 2021-22 under supply shock + demand stimulus), cycle extension mechanism breaks.

mechanism: OPEC+ spare capacity of 5-6 Mb/d caps crude oil prices and maintains oil contango, enabling the bifurcated signal (tight copper, loose oil) to persist. Without spare capacity, both would tighten simultaneously, re-creating a unified commodity cycle.
regime_dependence: Conditional on no major supply disruption (Strait of Hormuz, Libya). If spare capacity is drawn down below 2 Mb/d, bifurcation collapses.

mechanism: 12-24 month monetary transmission lags from 2022-23 tightening disproportionately affect bank-funded small caps (35-40% floating rate). Current small-cap distress may be a lagged tightening effect rather than a forward-looking deterioration signal.
regime_dependence: Operative while Fed funds rate remains elevated. If Fed eases and small-cap margins stabilize, transmission-lag interpretation is validated. If margins deepen despite easing, input-cost thesis gains.

mechanism: ERB decline is the first observable stage in the sequencing pattern (Stage 1). However, ERB's overstated utility (PPV 44-62%, 1-3 month lead time) means Stage 1 identification carries more uncertainty than the framework implies.
regime_dependence: Relationship holds across cycles but signal quality degrades when false positive rate is high. Most useful when combined with credit and breadth signals.

mechanism: Both reflect the same underlying regime uncertainty around the structural inflation floor (~2.3-2.8%). Gold's decoupling from real rates and stock-bond correlation flickering are parallel manifestations of the market's inability to price a stable inflation regime.
regime_dependence: Both persist while inflation expectations oscillate around the 2.5% regime threshold. Both would stabilize if inflation durably settles above or below this level.

mechanism: Passive flows may preserve historical drawdown magnitudes while distorting velocity. Index rebalancing mechanics could compress the Stage 1 to peak timeline or extend it, making historical 6-18 month timing unreliable.
regime_dependence: Increasingly relevant as passive share grows. At 55% AUM, velocity distortion is likely material but direction (acceleration vs extension) is uncertain.

mechanism: If the Kalecki fiscal floor is robust enough to prevent recession, the entire sequencing framework generates a historic false positive. If the floor is thin (0.3-0.6 multiplier), fiscal support may only delay, not prevent, the typical sequence.
regime_dependence: Depends on whether fiscal consolidation occurs and the state-dependent multiplier at that time. Floor is strongest during recession (multiplier 1.0-2.5) and thinnest at positive output gaps (0.3-0.6).

mechanism: R-star uncertainty creates divergent pricing across asset classes (bonds pricing r-star at 1.5-2.0%, equities pricing 0.5-1.0%). This divergence maps directly onto the stock-bond correlation instability as each asset class intermittently reprices toward the other's implicit r-star.
regime_dependence: Active while the Holston-Laubach-Williams 95% CI spans -0.5% to 2.5%. Resolves when r-star estimate narrows or markets converge on a single pricing.
